title: lippia carterae
intro: Lippia carterae, also known as the Carter's lippia or Carter's bush tea, is a plant species that belongs to the Lippia genus of flowering plants, commonly found in southern Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lippia carterae include its ability to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. Lippia carterae can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or it can be used in other herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
